rulings.Case Title: SHEREEN PINTO et al VS. M. GAEHWILER CONSTRUCTION,INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Index Of Evidence
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, September 8, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANTS JEREMY KOTAS, ANTHONY PANTALEONI, KOTAS/PANTALEONI ARCHITECTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Index Of Evidence Granted. Defendants have met their burden of establishing that the 10 year statute of limitations per CCP sec. 1375 expired on March 15, 2012, and Defendants were not added by a Doe amendment until April 9, 2013. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 24-25, 28.) Defendants have also met their initial burden of showing that the Doe amendment does not relate back to the filing of the original complaint because Plaintiffs cannot establish that they were genuinely ignorant of facts giving rise to Defendants' liability at the time the complaint was filed. (UMF 3-4, 8, 14-15, 17-20.) Plaintiffs have not raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether they were genuinely ignorant. Plaintiffs self-serving declarations that they were unaware of facts giving rise to Defendants' liability may be disregarded, as they are directly contradicted by the complaint and evidence. In the original complaint, Plaintiffs allege that "Defendants so negligently designed¿as to directly cause, create and/or contribute to the Defects and resulting damages and losses." (Complaint, pars. 19, 24.) It is undisputed Plaintiffs were aware that Defendants were the architects for the project at the time the complaint was filed. (UMF 3-4, 8.) Further it is undisputed that, prior to the complaint being filed, the attorney for Plaintiff 2189 Market Street Owners Association sent Defendants a demand letter threatening a lawsuit, as well as a letter seeking a tolling stipulation that also threatened a lawsuit. (UMF 14-15, 17-20.) Plaintiffs have "failed to demonstrate that at the time the complaint was filed [they were] ignorant of facts having a bearing upon the liability of [Defendants]." (Snoke v. Bolen (1991) 235 Cal.App.3d 1427, 1432.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: DIVYA CHABDLER VS. GREGG JOHNSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, September 8, 2014, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT DANIELLE LAZIER, DPPM, INC.'S Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Denied. Cross-Defendants have not set forth any evidence establishing that $7,000 is within the reasonable range of their liability. "If there is no substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of a settling defendant's liability, then a determination of good faith based upon such assumption is an abuse of discretion." (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350.) Further, Defendants have met their burden under CCP sec. 877.6(d) of showing that the settlement amount is not reasonable, in light of the fact that disclosures regarding the defects alleged in Plaintiff's complaint were made to Cross-Defendants. (Declaration of A. Jeanne Grove, Exs. 3-5.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY BRYANT VS. INVESTCO MANAGEMENT & DEVELOPMENT LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay This Action
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, September 8, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANTS STEVEN THOMPSON, DOUGLAS HANSON, INVESTCO MANAGEMENT & DEVELOPMENT LLC'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay This Action The court grants the motion to compel arbitration and Mr. Epsha's joinder and stays this action. Plaintiffs' attempt to plead around the binding arbitration clauses by only bringing tort claims and not naming AV21 LLC fails. Plaintiffs are obligated to arbitrate this dispute based on equitable estoppel and because of plaintiffs' binding agency and alter ego judicial admissions. Defendants sufficiently establish the arbitration agreements. Plaintiffs do not dispute the existence of the agreements. In Condee v. Longwood Mgmt. Corp. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 215, the court explains that (1) the party seeking arbitration does not need to introduce the agreement into evidence or provide the court with anything more than a copy of the agreement or a recitation of its terms; and (2) it is not necessary to follow the normal authentication procedures to show the arbitration agreement. Plaintiffs are basically suing defendants because defendants allegedly duped plaintiffs into buying securities of AV21 LLC. The evidence shows that plaintiffs were members of AV21 LLC and agreed to arbitrate any dispute arising out of the AV21 LLC operating contract (pars. 16.6 & 16.7 of operating contract). The evidence also demonstrates that AV21 LLC agreed to arbitrate any disputes with Investco M&D LLC regarding its management of AV21 LLC (par. 9 of management contract). Defendants correctly note that plaintiffs judicially admit that all of the individual defendants and LLC defendants are alter egos and agents of each other. (Complaint, pars. 7-10). Those judicial admissions are binding and create a basis for arbitration. The doctrine of estoppel also applies to this case. In Molecular Analytical Systems v. Ciphergen Biosystems, Inc. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 696, 715, the court explains: "Courts applying equitable estoppel against a signatory have 'looked to the relationships of persons, wrongs and issues, in particular whether the claims that the nonsignatory sought to arbitrate were " ' "intimately founded in and intertwined with the underlying contract obligations." ' " ' " [citation] Application of "the estoppel doctrine in this context does not require a conscious or subjective intent to avoid arbitration, but turns upon the nexus between the contract and the causes of action asserted." [citation] "The focus is on the nature of the claims asserted by the plaintiff against the nonsignatory defendant." [citation] "Claims that rely upon, make reference to, or are intertwined with claims under the subject contract are arbitrable." Plaintiffs' claims for securities fraud clearly arise from and are intertwined with the cited contracts. Plaintiff's other arguments to avoid arbitration lack merit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: RONALD BROOKS VS. KOBE ROYO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Answers To Judicial Council Form Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 8, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF RONALD BROOKS' Motion To Compel Further Answers To Judicial Council Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The plaintiff's motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion to compel Form Interrogatory 14.2 is DENIED as the further amended responses appear to be compliant. The motion to compel Form Interrogatory No. 15.1 is GRANTED. Within ten (10) days, the defendant is ordered to provide full and complete responses related to each affirmative defense pled. The plaintiff's request for sanctions in the amount of $660.00 is GRANTED. Payment shall be made within thirty (30) days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID WHITE VS. 540 LEAVENWORTH, LLC, AN CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Of Defendants' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 8, 2014, Line 2: Plaintiff David White's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The plaintiff's motion to compel responses to Special Interrogatory Nos. 1, 6, and 7 is GRANTED. The defendant did not waive objections. However, the defendant failed in its obligation to provide full and complete responses to a reasonable construction of the interrogatories. (See Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 71, 783.) The plaintiff's motion to compel responses to Request for Production of Documents Nos. 2, 3 and 13 is DENIED. The plaintiff's motion to compel responses to Request for Production of Documents Nos. 15, 18, 19, 20, 29, 30, 34, 35, and 38 is GRANTED. Within ten (10) days, the defendants are to provide full and complete responses to the interrogatories and document requests listed. Both parties' request for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT KUO VS. PALO ALTO NETWORKS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, September 8, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT PALO ALTO NETWORKS, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is overruled on the grounds that the Complaint fails to allege a viable basis for liability. The Complaint explicitly alleges that "following the acquisition, there was a substantial continuity of Cyvera's business operations, including but not limited to the use of the same employees, same locations, substantially same supervisory personnel, sale of substantially the same products and substantially same working conditions as prior to the sale." (Compl. 9.) Thus, Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged facts that support the imposition of successor liability. (See Cleveland v. Johnson (2012) 209 Cal.App. 4th 1315, 1334 ["If a corporation organizes another corporation with practically the same shareholders and directors, transfers all the assets but does not pay all the first corporation's debts, and continues to carry on the same business, the separate entities may be disregarded and the new corporation held liable for the obligations of the old."].) Next the Demurrer is sustained as to the cause of action for fraud, with 10 days leave to amend to allow Plaintiff to specifically allege who made the misrepresentations. The Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for negligent misrepresentation, fraudulent misrepresentation under California Labor Code section 970, breach of contract, and breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. The Complaint sufficiently alleges the requisite elements of each claim. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Plaintiff must allege the violation of a fundamental public policy, evidenced or tethered to some statutory or constitutional provision, that supports his claim. (Cf. Gould v. Maryland Sound Industries, Inc. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 1137, 1148 [holding that prompt payment of earned and unpaid wages was a fundamental public policy evidenced by multiple statutes].) The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the cause of action for promissory estoppel for Plaintiff to allege, if he can and in the alternative, facts supporting the lack of consideration of the employment contract. Finally, the Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the claim for unfair business practices in violation of Business and Professions Code section 17200. Plaintiff is required to plead facts establishing a cognizable claim to the "Commencement Option shares," rather than pleading a legal conclusion of ownership. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG
